name: Garrovillas de Alconétar from Alcántara – circular via Brozas and Navas del Madroño
name_original: Garrovillas de Alconétar desde Alcántara – circular por Brozas y Navas del Madroño
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alcántara traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and open plains. The region features a mix of agricultural land and scattered woodlands, providing varied scenery for road cyclists. Routes often follow well-maintained roads connecting small towns and historical sites. The terrain generally includes moderate ascents and descents, suitable for a range of cycling abilities. Best road cycling routes around Alcántara The most popular road cycling route is Garrovillas de Alconétar from Alcántara – circular via Brozas and Navas del Madroño, a 51.0 miles (82.1 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular journey through the local countryside, connecting several villages. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Salorino and Membrío from Brozas – circular by the río Salor, a moderate 50.2 miles (80.8 km) path. This route explores the area around the río Salor, featuring a mix of open and riverside scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Osborne Bull on the N-630 – Belvís Tower loop from Alcántara, a 72.4 miles (116.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Alcántara is defined by open plains, river valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including advanced road cycling routes and moderate paths. The routes in Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 160 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcántara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcántara?
A: There are over 9 road cycling routes around Alcántara, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include 7 moderate and 2 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Alcántara?
A: Road cycling routes around Alcántara traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and open plains. You'll find a mix of agricultural land and scattered woodlands, with generally moderate ascents and descents.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Alcántara?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6226522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roco-Campofrío Palace&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Almocóvar Church&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable settlement with historical charm.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints to discover while road cycling?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural attractions. You can cycle near the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the José María de Oriol - Alcántara II Dam&lt;/a&gt; for scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cantera de Alcántara Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brozas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; also offer picturesque spots.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for advanced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6911443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull on the N-630 – Belvís Tower loop from Alcántara&lt;/a&gt; offer a demanding 72.4 miles (116.6 km) journey with significant elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14706115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle – Puente de Alconétar loop from Garrovillas de Alconétar&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 77 miles (124 km).

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Alcántara?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Alcántara are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058751660/garrovillas-de-alconetar-from-alcantara-circular-via-brozas-and-navas-del-madrono" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garrovillas de Alconétar from Alcántara – circular via Brozas and Navas del Madroño&lt;/a&gt;, which is 51.0 miles (82.1 km) long.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Alcántara?
A: The road cycling routes in Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Over 160 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic countryside and well-maintained roads.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for endurance training?
A: For those looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14706115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabel Castle – Puente de Alconétar loop from Garrovillas de Alconétar&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, covering 77.1 miles (124 km). Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6911443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull on the N-630 – Belvís Tower loop from Alcántara&lt;/a&gt;, at 72.4 miles (116.6 km).

Q: Can I find routes that explore river valleys or specific natural features?
A: Yes, several routes highlight the region's natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058780024/salorino-and-membrio-from-brozas-circular-by-the-rio-salor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salorino and Membrío from Brozas – circular by the río Salor&lt;/a&gt; route specifically explores the area around the río Salor, offering a mix of open and riverside scenery.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alcántara?
A: While there are no routes explicitly classified as 'easy' in our current selection, many of the 'moderate' routes are suitable for cyclists with some experience. The terrain generally includes moderate ascents and descents, making them accessible for a range of abilities.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcántara?
A: Alcántara's climate generally allows for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the routes?
A: Many routes connect small towns and villages, providing opportunities to stop for refreshments. While specific cafes are not listed, these settlements often have local establishments where you can refuel and rest.
tips: Incredible views from the north side of the reservoir.

Built over the Tagus River near present-day Alcántara, in the province of Cáceres, the Alcántara Bridge is one of the most beautiful and important examples of Roman civil engineering. Despite being shorter than that of Mérida and with fewer arches, it is much higher and above all more impressive. The complex is made up of three distinct parts: the bridge, the central triumphal arch and a small rectangular temple measuring 5.60 x 4.10 meters. This building consists of a staircase, two Tuscan columns, an entablature and a classical pediment. A restored plaque indicates the name of its builder and reminds us that the bridge will last “as long as the world lasts”:

Ghostly ruins of a 15th century convent on the outskirts of Garrovillas de Alconétar. The convent of San Antonio de Padua was founded in 1476 but was left in ruins after being looted by the residents of the town in 1843.

Garrovillas de Alconétar is a small town in the Tajo-Salor region. A strategic place on the left bank of the Tagus River, near its confluence with the Almonte River. Stop at the Plaza Mayor for food and drinks and admire the heritage buildings that surround the Plaza.

Navas del Madroño is a small rural town located at the crossroads of three cattle ravines that lead to the capital Cáceres between the Alcántara and Alconétar bridges. Today, when you cycle to the city, don't forget to visit its most characteristic monuments, the Church of Nuestra Señora de la O, and stop for a coffee in the Plaza de Constitución.

The Plaza Mayor of Brozas (Cáceres, Extremadura) is the historic heart of this town, known for its rich heritage and traditional atmosphere. Although not as monumental as those of Cáceres or Trujillo, it has a special charm due to its history and vernacular architecture.

Brozas Castle, also known as “Castillo de la Comienda Mayor de la Orden de Alcántara” and popularly called “the Palace”, is a fortification located in the Spanish town of Brozas, in the province of Cáceres. Its history dates back to the 14th century, although its current appearance comes from a reconstruction carried out in the 16th-17th centuries.

Belvís Castle is a fortress located in the Spanish municipality of Villa del Rey, in the province of Cáceres, Extremadura. Built in the 13th century, only one tower currently remains. It was a commission from the Order of Alcántara of some relevance.

Some pieces of interest are preserved inside: the tomb of Commander Antonio Bravo of Jerez, made by Lucas Mitata; five panels by Luis de Morales; a Recumbent Christ, attributed to Martínez Montañés; a carving by Sebastián de Paz from 1639; the tomb of Master Yáñez de la Barbuda, and the font where San Pedro de Alcántara was baptized.
